sections.heading: Why Code Quality Matters
sections.content: High-quality code ensures maintainability, testability, readability, and resilience. Android apps evolve over time, and poor practices lead to bugs, regressions, and developer frustration. Following consistent best practices—clean architecture, clear naming, small focused classes, testing, and code reviews—makes codebases sustainable, productive, and enjoyable for years.

rows.description: UI logic in ViewModel, business logic in UseCases, data in Repositories
rows.anti_pattern: Network calls or business logic inside Activity/Fragment

rows.description: UI observes state; actions flow up to ViewModel, state flows down
rows.anti_pattern: UI modifies state directly or holds ViewModel references

rows.description: Inject dependencies via constructor; enhances testability
rows.anti_pattern: Instantiating Retrofit, Room, or Repositories inside classes

rows.description: Repository is the authoritative source for app data
rows.anti_pattern: Duplicating data in ViewModel and Fragment

sections.heading: Kotlin Best Practices
items: Prefer val over var for immutability
Use data classes and sealed classes for state modeling
Favor extension functions over utility classes
Use scope functions appropriately: let, apply, run, also, with
Avoid !!; prefer safe calls, Elvis operator, or require/checkNotNull
Use named arguments to avoid parameter confusion
Prefer when expressions over long if-else chains

sections.heading: ViewModel & Coroutine Practices
items: Expose immutable StateFlow; handle one-shot events via SharedFlow
Avoid Context references to prevent memory leaks
Use viewModelScope for coroutines; structured concurrency and correct Dispatchers
Use suspend functions over callbacks; Flow for streams
Handle cancellations and exceptions correctly

sections.heading: UI & Compose Practices
items: Keep composables stateless; lift state to ViewModel
Use state hoisting and remember/rememberSaveable appropriately
LazyColumn/LazyRow for lists; pass Modifier first
Avoid business logic inside composables

rows.description: Repository converts low-level errors to meaningful types

sections.heading: Code Review & CI/CD
items: Small, focused PRs with meaningful descriptions
Automate lint, ktlint, detekt in CI
Feature flags for incomplete features
Use GitFlow or trunk-based development; meaningful commit messages
Automate release builds via CI/CD; keep main branch releasable

sections.heading: Memory & Resource Management
items: Clear View bindings in onDestroyView(); avoid static Context references
Unregister listeners and observers properly
Use Glide/Coil for image loading with lifecycle awareness
Use LeakCanary for memory leak detection
Close resources explicitly

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Consistent application of Android best practices across architecture, Kotlin usage, ViewModel design, Compose, error handling, testing, CI/CD, and memory management ensures robust, maintainable, and scalable apps. Quality code results from disciplined small decisions made daily, not one-time efforts.
